source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Fire on Maersk Valiant, Alaminos Canyon 475

actions_taken: Crew mustered at primary muster locations
Fire Teams 1, 2, and 3 deployed to C-deck
Electrical and ventilation isolations performed for C-deck
Fire extinguishers discharged by Emergency Response Leader
Fire hose used to extinguish fire
All wall mounted fluorescent lights isolated
Rig orientation revised with safety presentation for personal electrical equipment
24-hour fire watch implemented in living quarters
All radios programmed with fire brigade channel
Safety Alerts sent by Maersk and ConocoPhillips
event.summary: On March 12, 2016, at 10:06 hours, a fire occurred in accommodation cabin 417 on the drill ship Maersk Valiant during drilling operations in Alaminos Canyon 475. The smoke alarm system was activated and personnel mustered. Fire Teams responded and extinguished the fire using fire hoses after handheld fire extinguishers proved ineffective. The fire was extinguished at 10:26 hours. Forensic analysis could not definitively identify the ignition source; the Samsung tablet could not be eliminated as a possible source but could not be proven. The source was classified as undetermined. Damage was estimated at $50,000 with no injuries reported. A similar fire had occurred in the B-deck linen closet on December 13, 2015.
recommendations: All electrical equipment must be checked in when arriving and inspected by electrical department
No electrical equipment should be left plugged into rig power system unattended
Charging devices should not be stored on materials with high combustion potential
24-hour fire watch in living quarters until well completion

hazards.name: Electrical fire hazard from personal electronic devices
Inadequate fire detection and response capability

threats.name: Unattended personal electrical equipment left plugged into power system
Electrical device malfunction or arcing in confined space
Charging devices stored on high-combustion materials
Delayed fire response due to communication breakdown
Delayed fire suppression due to fire hydrant valve malfunction

consequences.name: Fire in accommodation cabin with potential for personnel injury

controls.name: Smoke alarm system
controls.lod_basis: Early warning system for fire detection

supporting_text: At 10:01, the rig smoke alarm system was activated, and the Offshore Installation Manager announced for all personnel to proceed to their primary muster locations.

controls.name: Fire Teams and emergency response procedures
controls.lod_basis: Trained personnel response to fire emergency

supporting_text: Fire Teams 1, 2, and 3 were deployed to the C-deck to extinguish the fire.

The initial Responder to cabin 417, who was also the Emergency Response Leader, had taken a radio which did not have the fire brigade channel. Fire teams awaiting instructions were unable to communicate via radio and get clear directions from the Emergency Response Leader.
Prior to a Fire Team getting to C-deck cabin 417, two fire extinguishers were discharged by the Emergency Response Leader into cabin 417 which did not extinguish the fire.
The initial response with fire extinguishers could not put out the fire, and fire hoses were required to completely extinguish the fire.

controls.name: Fixed water fire suppression system (fire hoses)

supporting_text: At 10:19, a Fire Team entered C-deck accommodation cabin 417 and began to extinguish the fire with a fire hose. After the use of a fire hose, the fire was finally extinguished at 10:26.
The fire hydrant valve spring did not release to charge fire hose immediately. Personnel had to strike the valve with a spanner wrench to charge hose

controls.name: Electrical and ventilation isolation procedures
controls.barrier_role: Containment and prevention of fire spread
controls.lod_basis: Isolation of electrical sources and ventilation to limit fire propagation

supporting_text: Electrical and ventilation isolations were performed for the C-deck.

controls.name: Prohibition on unattended personal electrical equipment
controls.barrier_role: Prevention of ignition source from personal devices
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent hazardous electrical equipment use

supporting_text: Preliminary findings suggested a wall mounted fluorescent light for marine use with receptacle, a universal adapter plugged into the light fixture, an Apple I-Phone ice cube type phone charger plugged into the adapter, and/ or a Samsung tablet left under the bunk pillow may have started the fire.
No electrical equipment should be left plugged into the rig power system unattended at any time.

controls.name: Electrical equipment inspection and control procedures
controls.barrier_role: Prevention of defective electrical equipment introduction
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to inspect and manage electrical equipment

supporting_text: Safety measures have been implemented for electronic devices to include that all electrical equipment must be checked in when arriving and inspected by the electrical department.

controls.name: Proper storage of charging devices away from combustible materials
controls.barrier_role: Prevention of ignition of combustible materials
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent heat exposure to combustibles

supporting_text: a Samsung tablet left under the bunk pillow may have started the fire
Charging devices should not be stored on materials which have a high potential for combustion when exposed to relatively low heat source.

controls.name: Radio communication system with fire brigade channel
controls.lod_basis: Communication system for coordinated fire response

supporting_text: The initial Responder to cabin 417, who was also the Emergency Response Leader, had taken a radio which did not have the fire brigade channel. Fire teams awaiting instructions were unable to communicate via radio and get clear directions from the Emergency Response Leader.
All radios after the incident have been programmed with the fire brigade channel.

controls.name: Cabin assignment and occupancy control
controls.barrier_role: Prevention of unauthorized occupancy and equipment storage
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control of cabin assignments and use

supporting_text: Cabin 417 was supposed to be an extra cabin with no personnel assigned. However, a Stewardess had asked permission to stay in the room. The Stewardess' assigned room number was not changed and most of her belongings were not transferred to cabin 417.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
